name: Bencisco
description: The Bencisco method is an intermediate FTO (Face-Turning Octahedron) solving technique created by Ben Streeter in 2018. It structures the FTO solve into block building, center solving, and corner-center pair insertion. This method provides a clear and logical framework for tackling the FTO puzzle efficiently.

steps.name: Build Block
steps.description: Build an initial block on the FTO by combining connected pieces into a solved section. This block serves as the anchor for the rest of the solve and is built intuitively.
tips: Plan the block during inspection for a fast start
Choose the block position based on which pieces are already connected
Efficient block building is the most important skill for this method
Practice building blocks from different starting positions

steps.name: Solve Centers
steps.description: Solve the remaining center pieces of the FTO without disturbing the built block. Centers are placed using short algorithmic sequences or intuitive moves.
tips: Learn how FTO moves affect centers specifically
Use the unsolved portions of the puzzle for setup moves
Solve centers in an order that minimizes disruption
Some centers can be solved simultaneously with efficient move sequences

steps.name: Corner-Center Pairs
steps.description: Solve the remaining pieces by pairing corners with their adjacent centers and inserting them into their correct positions. This is similar in concept to F2L on a 3x3 but adapted to the FTO's octahedral geometry.
tips: Learn the common pair cases and their insertions
Look ahead to the next pair while inserting the current one
Pair and insert efficiently to minimize move count
Practice recognizing pair cases from different viewing angles

pros: Logical and structured approach to the FTO
Block building provides a strong foundation
Accessible for solvers transitioning from 3x3 methods
Good balance of intuition and algorithmic solving
cons: Block building efficiency varies significantly between solves
FTO geometry is unfamiliar to most cubers initially
creator: Ben Streeter
